questions.question: Define the statement ¬(P ∧ Q), where P and Q are propositions. Provide a brief explanation of its meaning in natural language.
questions.hint: Start with the definition of ¬ (negation) and ∧ (conjunction)

questions.question: Prove the statement: If P implies Q, then Q implies P. Use a logical diagram or truth table to support your proof.
questions.hint: Use the definition of implication and the commutative law of implication

questions.question: Define the statement (P ∨ Q) ∧ R, where P, Q, and R are propositions. Explain why this statement is equivalent to P ∧ (Q ∨ R) in terms of truth value.
questions.hint: Start with the definition of ∨ (disjunction) and ∧ (conjunction)
